signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ Hike Risk
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -13,767 (specs fading hawkishness) vs RBNZ hike bias following a 3:3 split vote and Kiwibank's July hike call. Real rate at -0.83% remains the most accommodative in the G8, but shifting OIS pricing for the July 8 meeting creates acute short-squeeze risk. Crowded spec shorts face capitulation if inflation fears from oil supply shocks persist. NZD/USD at 0.5933.
signals.watch level: Next RBNZ meeting on 8 July; watch for 0.6000 psychological resistance break.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry + Options Skew
signals.text: USD/JPY at 159.704 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid despite Iran-related safe-haven headlines. Options market shows a moderate put skew (25d RR at -1.06), signaling institutional hedging against BoJ intervention as price tests the 160.00 threshold. JPY COT LF net short -86,249 confirms a crowded carry trade. Carry dominance persists while VIX remains at 16.1 (normal volatility).
signals.watch level: 160.00 level for BoJ verbal or physical intervention; next BoJ meeting 16 Jun.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol + CESI Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7159 — COT LF net long +58,041 (76.1% long) represents a positioning extreme while CESI net -5 misses indicates deteriorating macro fundamentals. Options market is pricing a massive 8.5 vol-pt premium (IV 16.5% vs HV30 8.0%), suggesting high demand for downside protection. Retail 75% short bias diverges from institutional longs, increasing the risk of a sharp long-unwind if data continues to miss.
signals.watch level: Next RBA meeting 16 Jun; watch for break of 0.7100 support.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Volatility Premium + Funding Stress
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7864 — Volatility Risk Premium (VRP) at +11.7% (IV 18.0% vs HV30 6.3%) marks the highest in the G8 complex, signaling extreme hedging demand. DXY vs SPX correlation at -0.601 (z=+1.71σ) indicates emerging USD funding stress as safe-haven bids shift to the Greenback over CHF. Fed 3.75% vs SNB 0% (+375bp) carry advantage remains the structural floor for the pair.
signals.watch level: Next SNB meeting 18 Jun; watch for SARON OIS shifts.
